ab: I tosa lefot tosa polo’ ira ko tosa a mihecaan, midemak ko seyfo to kayki o nga’ayay to a pakafiten ko ngangan no Yencomin i nifonsiw.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列名字的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: Mihomong kako ci Fadasan o milongcay to sefo to pipakafit to ngangan no Yencomini i nifonsiw a kapah no niyaro’ cira.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Fadas
en: I interviewed Fadas, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: Matatelek to kako ato mira tona pa’aliwan a mihomong ciraan.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: Toya pihomongan i, siwkayen nira kako to sakaira no ngangan nira, mipakafana’ haca to laloma’an nira, niyaro’ nira.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: Licayen ako cira, mana pacici sa cira a mifalic to ngangan, ya matelangay a mitingkian a ngangan, cowa kanga’ay haw?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: Sowal han nira kako, cowa kamoecel ko tilid no Kowaping to todong no ngangan nira. Anca i, “Fa syen sen” hananay no tao cira, saka adihay ko kalatawaan nona pipangiha’ to ngangan.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「發先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Fa Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: Sowal sa ci Fadas, “O no Yencomin ko mitilidan a ngangan nga, o tadangangan no mita. O sakatatodong no finacadan ita, laloma’an ita, tireng ita ko ngangan ako. Saka, nanay ’aloman ko patikolay to niyah to a ngangan.”
ch: Fadas說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Fadas said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
